extra.message: User input from 'name' parameter is directly interpolated into SQL string using % formatting operator. The SQL query string is constructed as: q = ("INSERT INTO students (name) " "VALUES ('%(name)s')") % {'name': name}. This allows SQL injection via the name parameter.

Jinja2 template autoescape is explicitly disabled with autoescape=False. This allows user-controlled data rendered in templates to execute as JavaScript code, leading to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Password verification uses MD5 hashing which is cryptographically broken. The check_password method compares pwd_hash == md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest(). MD5 is vulnerable to collision attacks and rainbow table lookups. Use bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Review text from review.review_text is rendered without HTML escaping. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks through stored review content.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Course description from course.description is rendered without HTML escaping. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks through stored course descriptions.

extra.message: Database credentials (username 'postgres' and password 'postgres') are hardcoded in the configuration file config/dev.yaml. This is a security anti-pattern that exposes database access credentials to anyone with access to the source code.
